reviews.review_description: This game is more like shooting revolving ducks. The dinosaurs look great but they just run around back and forth until you kill them. They didn’t even try to attack much. Had a T Rex step on me and no damage done. Glitchy too. Hard to control your hands at times. Needs improvement.

The dinosaurs in this game look incredibly realistic,.. and the overall ambience is fantastic... I really enjoyed interacting with the T-Rex hunting it down genuinely gave me chills. The shooting mechanics are solid, though they could be a bit more refined and mature. Still, if someone is looking for a realistic environment and an intense dinosaur-hunting experience in VR, I would definitely recommend this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your feedback! We’re really glad to hear that the dinosaurs, ambience, and the T-Rex encounters gave you an immersive experience. It means a lot to our team.

We also appreciate your thoughts on the shooting mechanics!. we’re actively working on refining and maturing the combat feel in upcoming updates. Reviews like yours help us improve and push the game to the next level.

Thanks again for the recommendation and for playing our game! Your support truly motivates us. 🙌🦖

reviews.review_description:
This Dino Hunting VR game delivers a seriously immersive experience....The graphics are extremely realistic  from the detailed dinosaur textures to the lush environment, everything looks stunning inside the headset.... Hunting these massive creatures with a bow and arrow is genuinely exciting and feels intense during close encounters...
However, the bow-and-arrow mechanics could be improved a bit to make aiming and shooting feel smoother and more responsive. If that gets polished, this game can easily become one of the best VR dinosaur hunting experiences on the Meta Store....
Overall, a fun and visually impressive game with great potential!..

reviews.review_title: Must have 4 Dino fans! Combat need works
reviews.review_description: WOAH! 🤯This game makes me feel like I’m in the TV show, Walking With Dinosaurs! 🤩 but the actual “hunting” part is basically broken. I would keep trying to grab the arrows with the grip button but nothing happens till like the 6-8th attempt. And if you accidentally drop the bow or sword and shield, you have to scoop down and reach ground level for the weapons. Even then, you still gotta attempt to grab 6-8 times. Also, when either the shield or sword are dropped, they slide away from your hand like as if they were kicked. Super frustrating. Furthermore, when dinosaurs are shot with an arrorw, they’d die without notice moments later. But what’s making I keep the game is actually the amazing immersive experience being able to walk by and underneath the dinosaurs! Perhaps the developers should consider pivoting this game as a safari experience instead of an action game. The visuals and immersive experience is just amazing!

developer_response.body:
Hey! 👋
Thanks so much for the detailed feedback  we really appreciate it! We’re glad to hear you enjoyed the immersive “walking with dinosaurs” experience 🦖✨. Regarding the grabbing and weapon interaction issues, we’re actively working on improving VR hand detection and object pickup responsiveness for a smoother experience.

Your suggestion about focusing more on the safari-style exploration is awesome ! it’s something we’re seriously considering for future updates! Thanks again for sharing your thoughts and helping us make the game better. 🙌

reviews.review_description: It really feels like you’ve stepped back into the age of dinosaurs!.. the environment and the creatures look incredibly realistic.... Overall, the game is very enjoyable, but adding a sniper mode would make a huge difference and take it to the next level...

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing feedback! We're really happy to hear that the environment and dinosaurs made you feel truly immersed in that ancient world. Creating a realistic experience is something we put a lot of love into.

Your suggestion about adding a sniper mode is great.  it’s actually something we've been discussing internally. Hearing it directly from players like you motivates us even more to explore it for future updates.

Thanks again for playing and for helping us make the game even better! 🦖🎯

reviews.review_description: The handling of weapons is really sketchy and glitchy. Sometimes you can't even grab them. Sometimes they just fly away when you reach out for them. If you drop them on the ground they probably just disappear if you try to grab them again. At least the arrows do. There is no survival mode. It's just basically free hunt and there's one table with weapons on them. If I could return it I would

developer_response.body:
Thank you! this is very helpful feedback.

Sir, we’re continuously working to improve the game. Apologies for the frustrating experience! that’s not the play we want players to have. A few quick notes that should help right away: if you point at a weapon and press the grip button, the weapon should automatically come to your hand (you don’t need to bend down or search for it). We’re aware of issues where arrows or dropped weapons sometimes disappear or behave oddly, and we’re actively investigating and fixing those glitches.

We’ll address weapon handling, grabbing stability, and the item-drop bugs in an upcoming update, and we’re also looking at better game modes and balance based on player feedback.

If you’re willing, please send us your headset model and a short description of when the problem happens (e.g., which level,). That will help us pinpoint and prioritize the fix faster. Thanks again for reporting this. we really appreciate it and are on it.

Ascendrix Studio

reviews.review_description: Is it a bad game? not inherently. Is it bad for the price tag it asks for? I believe so. Getting this game on sale for $6 still doesn’t feel worth it, let alone the original price tag. It might be a bit nit picky but the scaling of the dinosaurs isn’t the most accurate although the brachiosaurus, t rex, spinosaurus, and stegosaurus were decently accurate. The environment is a bit bland and doesnt make the best use of the available space, dinosaurs feel a little clumped together when spawned, not to mention most of the time you are wandering into the lions den just to actually get your weapons. There is a decent collection of dinosaurs but all lack a unique ability, a dilophasuarus does as much damage as a t rex, they generally all just range in speed, maybe a pteranodon would be a cool edition to add variety of animal. Maybe have a small little cabin you spawn in with the weapons that at least lets you prep, that way instead of rushing in you can carry multiple weapons in with their own ammo. I am a firm believer that a game doesn’t have to be incredibly detailed to be good but the gameplay has to be fun or immersive, the pistol is too weak and the shotgun is so hard to properly function with a lack of carrying ammo and and the assault rifle being the most powerful that you get for free makes it really the only choice which when mixed with the same exact mission of killing a dinosaur taking the exact same amount of shots over and over can make gameplay quickly repetitive and the insane speed you move at that you can’t seem to change makes it really hard to immerse with. Its very possible weapons are holsterable but I wouldn’t know with a lack of game settings or any kind of tutorial. I will reiterate, this game is not inherently bad, I just think there is far better use you could make of the money being asked for here.

The graphics in this game are absolutely incredible…the textures on the dinos and the environment look unbelievable real, almost like u are standing inside a living, breathing world…this is one of the best looking vr games I have played on the store. Highly recommended!

It's horrible to play. I can't even begin to explain. You have a bag but can't store ammo? Just weapons. I thought it was a good idea and looked nice but honestly it's not much of a game and no settings, nothing
